title: Health Care Affordability Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes it easier for families to get help paying for health insurance. Right now, families can only get financial help if they make less than a certain amount of money, but this bill removes that limit so more families can qualify. The bill also keeps lower payment amounts that help people pay less for their health insurance. These changes will be permanent and start in 2026.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Health Care Affordability Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Increase in Eligibility for Credit
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to help more people get financial help to buy health insurance. Right now, families can only get help if they make less than four hundred percent of the poverty line. This bill removes that limit so families who make more money can also get help. The bill also sets up a new system for how much families have to pay for health insurance based on their income. Families making up to one hundred fifty percent of the poverty line pay nothing. Families making between one hundred fifty and two hundred percent pay between zero and two percent of their income. Families making between two hundred and two hundred fifty percent pay between two and four percent. Families making between two hundred fifty and three hundred percent pay between four and six percent. Families making between three hundred and four hundred percent pay between six and eight and a half percent. Families making four hundred percent or more pay eight and a half percent of their income. The amount each family pays increases gradually within each income range. The bill also removes some old rules that are no longer needed. These changes will start for tax years beginning after December thirty first twenty twenty five.
